items.title: Larceny Act 1916
items.summary: The Larceny Act 1916 consolidates and simplifies laws on larceny (theft), including definitions of stealing, specific forms like robbery, burglary, embezzlement, receiving stolen goods, and related offences such as demanding with menaces, with graded punishments, procedural rules, and repeals of prior statutes.
items.reason: Property rights are the bedrock of liberty, wealth creation, and individualism; without statutory criminalization and punishment of theft, robbery, burglary, and fraud, owners would face rampant violations, deterring investment, production, and exchange, leading to economic stagnation and reduced prosperity. Deleting this would leave critical protections to fragmented common law, eroding incentives and institutions essential for a free society, making citizens demonstrably worse off as unseen costs of insecurity outweigh any regulatory burdens.
